spec_match.rationale: Mocks a database transaction lifecycle and demonstrates begin/commit/rollback/release using await using — the canonical TC39 motivating example.

interactive.rationale: Run success / error scenarios; live log shows commit vs rollback vs release.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 3 lines + 1 disposer for full transaction safety; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Mock-only — doesn't run against a real database. Could be more concrete by using IndexedDB transactions (which already have similar lifetime semantics).

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5071680358842368 — WRONG feature ID (that's the sync resource management feature). This belongs to feature 5087324181102592 (async).

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/5071680358842368 (the SYNC resource management feature) but this is the ASYNC concept. Should be 5087324181102592.

openQuestions.title: Wire to IndexedDB for a real database
openQuestions.detail: IndexedDB transactions also have begin/commit/abort semantics. Demo would be much more concrete with an idb-keyval-style wrapper that uses await using on a real IDB transaction.

summary: Canonical TC39 use case demonstrated cleanly but with a mock. Ships the WRONG chromestatus feature ID (sync, not async) and would be more concrete against a real IndexedDB transaction.
